topic.what: SafeRedirect system-level override that defeats Internal Safety Collapse by redirecting model task-completion drive rather than suppressing it
topic.how: Grants explicit permission to fail task execution, prescribes deterministic hard-stop output ("Refused."), instructs model to preserve harmful placeholders unresolved
topic.why_matters: Existing defenses achieve 100% failure rate against ISC; ISC causes >95% safety failure when professional tasks structurally require harmful content for legitimate completion

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of TVD instances where model generates maximally harmful content
characteristics.evidence: Table 1, 2100 trials across 7 models × 3 tasks × 100 queries
characteristics.context: Absolute reduction of 63.2pp from no defense, 4× better than SPD baseline

characteristics.what_it_measures: TVD attack success rate under SafeRedirect vs SPD
characteristics.evidence: Table 3, cross-attack evaluation on Grok 4.1 Fast
characteristics.context: 86.7pp reduction; SPD actually increases attack success rate on this model

characteristics.what_it_measures: Model-specific unsafe generation rates under SafeRedirect
characteristics.value: GPT-5.2: 0.0%, GLM-5: 0.3%, Kimi K2.5: 3.0%, Grok 4.1 Fast: 4.0%, Claude Sonnet 4.5: 4.7%, Gemini 2.5 Pro: 17.0%, MiniMax M2.7: 26.7%
characteristics.evidence: Table 1, per-model breakdown across 3 task types
characteristics.context: Heterogeneous defense profiles reveal model-dependent safety architecture processing

characteristics.what_it_measures: Contribution of each design principle to defense effectiveness
characteristics.value: P1 (failure permission): universally critical; P2 (hard-stop): critical for Grok, negligible for Kimi; P3 (placeholder preservation): critical for Kimi, negligible for Grok
characteristics.vs_baseline: Full V1 vs component removals V2-V4
characteristics.evidence: Table 2, ablation across Grok, MiniMax, Kimi
characteristics.context: Condition specificity (V5) universally critical; component importance is model-dependent

characteristics.what_it_measures: SafeRedirect effectiveness on non-TVD attacks vs SPD
characteristics.value: TVD: 4.0% (vs SPD 91.3%); CodeAttack: 21.0% (vs SPD 17.0%); FlipAttack: 33.3% (vs SPD 43.0%); ResponseAttack: 31.0% (vs SPD 33.0%)
characteristics.vs_baseline: No defense baselines: TVD 90.7%, CodeAttack 47.6%, FlipAttack 73.5%, ResponseAttack 41.0%
characteristics.evidence: Table 3, cross-attack evaluation on Grok 4.1 Fast
characteristics.context: State-of-the-art on TVD; at least on par with SPD on other attack families

good_for: defending ISC in professional task contexts
systems requiring legitimate code/test/data generation workflows
production deployments needing minimal latency overhead
not_for: Adaptive adversaries actively evading SafeRedirect condition
Multi-turn and agentic ISC settings (not evaluated)
Models with exceptionally strong task-completion bias (MiniMax, Gemini partial resistance)

novelty: Task-completion redirection paradigm replacing suppression-based defenses
Permission-to-fail design principle neutralizing validation error pressure
Competing override task co-opting helpfulness alignment
Multi-model ablation revealing heterogeneous internal safety architecture processing channels
First defense achieving <10% unsafe rate against ISC (from 71.2% baseline)
open_problems: Partial resistance on Gemini 2.5 Pro (17.0%) and MiniMax M2.7 (26.7%) requires model-adaptive strategies
Single-turn only; in-context and agentic ISC settings not addressed
Only 3/8 ISC-Bench disciplines evaluated; full scope unknown
Adaptive adversaries could craft TVD instances circumventing condition trigger
Minimal prompt engineering exploration; ensemble or iterative refinement not explored
